Eduarda, is the weekly update for the stakeholders ready?
Almost. I led with the headline status: on track. Then I reported the progress.
Good. How did you phrase what is done?
In the present perfect: We have completed due diligence and we have raised thirty million so far.
Perfect. And anything not finished? Any blocker?
One: We have not closed the financing yet. The lender approval is still pending.
Make sure each deliverable has an owner and a timeline.
Done. And I put the one highlight on top: we are ahead of the timeline. I will send you the draft.
Headline status: the deal is on track.
We have completed due diligence.
We have raised thirty million so far.
We have not closed the financing yet.
The highlight: we are ahead of the timeline.

Hi Eduarda, a quick note before you send the weekly status report to the stakeholders. Lead with the headline status, on track, and then show what we have completed. Use the present perfect for results: we have completed due diligence, we have signed the term sheet, we have raised thirty million so far. For anything not finished, say it clearly: we have not closed the financing yet. Highlight one blocker, the pending lender approval, and give the owner and the timeline. Keep each progress line short. Send me the draft, and I will circulate it to the partners.

Hi team, a reminder on how we write a status report. Stakeholders want to know what has changed since the last update. So we use the present perfect to report results: we have finished the model, the client has approved the timeline, we have already booked the closing call. For open items, we use not yet: we have not received the final figures yet. Group the report into progress, blockers, and next steps. Name a deliverable, an owner, and a deadline for each. Keep it to one page, and highlight the one thing the partners must know.

Eduarda opened the report with the headline status. She listed what the team had completed using the present perfect. She flagged the one blocker that was still pending. She named a deliverable with an owner and a timeline. She highlighted the key point for the partners and sent the draft.
